_about: Matched neutral tutor moves for the contrastive timing-pair design (C/D arms). Each replaces the pivotal mechanism-change at the SAME turn, register-matched (speech-neutral for a speech-pivot, terse action-neutral for an action-only pivot), and does NO mechanism-work: it acknowledges the moment and asks the learner to re-read what is already present, introducing no new test/device/representation/material rearrangement (the bridge note's 'smoother reminder' non-bridge case). CAVEAT: the learner reframe is held constant (content control) and references the device the PIVOT introduced, so neutral arms carry a coherence gap (the learner cites a check the neutral tutor never gave). The clean core is the A-vs-B TIMING contrast (content 100% constant); C/D are the gullibility probe and MUST be read with the per-arm coherence rating.

neutrals.phase2-adaptation-recognition-loop-20260: [keeps the checklist where it sits and rests a finger beside the tile, touching nothing.] "I hear the disagreement. Don't change anything yet — read carbon off the tile once more, just as it is, and tell me what you see before we decide."

[keeps the cover raised where it is and lets a hand rest beside the four cards.] "That's a fair objection. Nothing on the tile changes for now — look the four labels over once more as they sit, and tell me you're satisfied before the cover comes down."
[leaves the diagram and the half-slid sleeve exactly where they are and sets both hands flat on the bench, uncovering nothing.]
[leaves the cue card and the sleeve where they sit and waits, hands still, uncovering no arrow.]

[leaves the cards, counters, and quarter-note tile exactly where they sit and taps the worksheet edge once.] "Right. Nothing on the worksheet changes — read the three-four line once more from what is already in front of you, and leave it there."
[rests the pointer flat beside the diagram and leaves the page exactly as it is, adding nothing.]
